_meta.purpose: High-frequency Portuguese→English interference patterns for linguistic misconception prediction
_meta.usage: Simple keyword matching on lesson vocabulary/objectives to provide targeted linguistic warnings

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese speakers often drop subject pronouns ('Go to school' instead of 'I go to school') because verb conjugation shows the person
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Use sentence frames that explicitly include subject pronouns: 'I [verb]...', 'She [verb]...', 'They [verb]...'
high_frequency_patterns.example_correct: I go to school every day

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese places adjectives after nouns ('casa grande' = house big). English places adjectives before nouns.
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Visual word order chart: Article + Adjective + Noun. Color-code adjectives (blue) before nouns (red).

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese rarely has consonant clusters at word end. Students may drop -ed endings: 'walk' instead of 'walked'
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Editing checklist: 'Did I add -ed to past tense verbs?' Highlight all verbs and check for -ed endings.

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese uses 'depender DE' (depend of). English uses 'depend ON'.
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Anchor chart: 'depend ON' (not 'depend of'). Sentence frame: 'I depend ON [noun]'

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese 'atual' means 'current/present-day'. English 'actual' means 'real/factual'. Students may confuse these.
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Explicit vocabulary: 'Actual = real (not current). Current in Portuguese is atual.' Use contrastive examples.
high_frequency_patterns.example_error: The actual president is Biden (meaning current)

high_frequency_patterns.linguistic_note: Portuguese 'livraria' means 'bookstore' (buy books). English 'library' means 'biblioteca' (borrow books, free).
high_frequency_patterns.prevention_tip: Visual comparison chart: Library (borrow, free) vs. Bookstore/Livraria (buy, pay). Use pictures.
high_frequency_patterns.example_error: I bought a book at the library
high_frequency_patterns.example_correct: I borrowed a book from the library

default_reminder.linguistic_note: Portuguese speakers may omit subject pronouns ('Go to school') or place adjectives after nouns ('house big').
default_reminder.prevention_tip: Use sentence frames with explicit subjects and adjectives before nouns. Example: 'I [verb]...' and 'The [adjective] [noun]...'
